title: Classification of ODE Solutions by ELC(R) Membership
summary: Linear constant-coefficient ODEs produce solutions in ELC(R). Linear variable-coefficient ODEs and nonlinear ODEs often require special functions (Bessel, Airy, hypergeometric) that lie outside ELC(R).

boundary_theorem.statement: Solutions of linear constant-coefficient ODEs are in ELC(R). Solutions requiring special functions (Bessel, Airy, hypergeometric) are outside ELC(R).
boundary_theorem.inside_class: Exp-polynomial: finite sums of terms c_i * x^{k_i} * exp(lambda_i * x), lambda_i in C. Real ELC excludes oscillatory (sin/cos) terms; complex ELC includes them.
boundary_theorem.outside_class: Bessel functions J_n(x), Y_n(x); Airy functions Ai(x), Bi(x); hypergeometric functions 2F1; Mathieu functions; Lame functions; any function not expressible via finite composition of exp, log, arithmetic.

classifications.solution_form: y = exp(-∫p dx) * [∫q*exp(∫p dx) dx + C]
classifications.condition: p, q must themselves be ELC-computable
classifications.notes: Integrating factor is exp of an integral; if p is ELC-computable the result stays in ELC.

classifications.example_cost_breakdown: exp (1n) + scalar multiply by r (1n) + scalar multiply by y0 (1n) = 3n
classifications.notes: Simplest ELC member; canonical benchmark for cost-1 base.

classifications.example_cost_breakdown: neg (1n) + mul (1n) + exp (1n) + add-1 (1n) + reciprocal (1n) + mul-K (1n) + shift constant (1n) = 7n
classifications.notes: Nonlinear ODE but exactly solvable via separation; solution is a sigmoid, cost 7n.

classifications.membership: OUTSIDE real ELC(R)  |  INSIDE complex ELC(C)
classifications.notes: sin and cos are outside ELC(R) (T01 classification). Via Euler: exp(i*omega*t) is in ELC(C), so solutions lie in ELC(C) but require imaginary exponents. Real ELC cannot represent periodic oscillations.

classifications.membership: MIXED — envelope INSIDE ELC(R), oscillatory factor OUTSIDE real ELC(R)
classifications.notes: The decay envelope exp(-alpha*t) is ELC-computable. Multiplying by sin/cos produces a function outside real ELC. Critically damped case (omega=0): y = (A+Bt)*exp(-alpha*t) — fully INSIDE ELC(R), cost ~5n.

classifications.example_cost_breakdown: neg (1n) + div (1n) + exp (1n) + scalar mul A (1n) + add T_inf (1n) = 5n
classifications.notes: Exponential decay to ambient temperature; direct ELC member.

Bessel functions are defined by infinite series or integral representations with no finite exp-log closed form. Half-integer orders (n=1/2) reduce to sin/cos/sqrt combinations — still outside real ELC(R) due to trig. Not expressible as finite composition of exp, log, arithmetic.

Airy functions arise from the Bessel equation of order 1/3; they have integral representations (contour integrals of exp) but no finite exp-log formula. The variable coefficient x in the ODE drives the solution outside ELC.

cases.example: y' = 1 + y^2  →  y = tan(x)  — OUTSIDE real ELC(R) (trig); but y' = -y^2 + 1  →  y = tanh(x) — INSIDE ELC(R), cost ~7n

cases.case: Reduces to Bessel equation via substitution
cases.example: General Riccati with variable coefficients often transforms to a second-order linear ODE whose solutions are Bessel functions.

classifications.notes: The Riccati equation is solvable in closed form only when a particular solution is known. With constant coefficients and real characteristic roots, solutions are tanh or coth — INSIDE ELC(R).

classifications.solution_form: Reduces to linear via substitution v = y^(1-n)
classifications.notes: The substitution v = y^(1-n) linearizes the equation to v' + (1-n)*p(x)*v = (1-n)*q(x), which is a first-order linear ODE solvable by integrating factor. The solution inherits the ELC membership of the linear case.

constant_coefficient_boundary.rule: Linear ODEs with constant coefficients have characteristic equations with algebraic roots. Solutions are exp-polynomial (sum of x^k * exp(lambda*x) terms). All such solutions are in ELC(R) when eigenvalues are real, or ELC(C) when complex.
constant_coefficient_boundary.rationale: The characteristic polynomial factoring produces only exp-type basis functions. No new transcendentals are introduced.

variable_coefficient_boundary.rule: Linear ODEs with non-constant (variable) coefficients typically require special functions (Bessel, Airy, Whittaker, hypergeometric, Mathieu). These are outside ELC(R) because they cannot be expressed as finite compositions of exp and log.
variable_coefficient_boundary.exceptions: Euler-Cauchy equation x^2*y'' + ax*y' + by = 0 reduces via substitution x = e^t to a constant-coefficient ODE — solutions are x^lambda (power functions), which ARE in ELC(R).

nonlinear_boundary.rule: Nonlinear ODEs: solvable by separation or exact methods sometimes yield ELC solutions (logistic, Bernoulli). Nonlinear ODEs lacking closed-form solutions (Lorenz, Navier-Stokes) are outside ELC by definition.
nonlinear_boundary.notes: Tanh and sigmoid appear as exact solutions to simple nonlinear ODEs and are both in ELC(R).

outside_elc_summary: Bessel functions J_n(x), Y_n(x) — Bessel equation
Airy functions Ai(x), Bi(x) — Airy equation
Sine and cosine — harmonic oscillator (real ELC only)
Riccati with variable coefficients reducing to Bessel
